title: Tesla, Alphabet stock prices plunge on Q2 earnings, AI capex spending
excerpt: Tesla and Alphabet shares crumbled on Thursday. Soaring capex has spooked investors, who are eager to see AI projects finally yield some return.
content_html:
Spending strikes again: Tesla and Alphabet are being punished by investors for lofty capex plans

By Samuel O'Brient

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Gone are the days when investors went along cheerily with Big Tech's plans to spend enormous sums on AI projects.

That fact was on display with the precipitous post-earnings declines in shares of Tesla and Alphabet on Thursday. Elon Musk's EV maker saw its stock drop almost 15%, while the Google parent fell nearly 7%.

Tesla and Alphabet both reported earnings for the second quarter on Wednesday. Tesla boosted its capex projection for the year to over $25 billion, with Musk describing this year's spending as "massive" on the call with investors. Meanwhile, Alphabet bumped up its capex projection from $180 billion to $190 billion, to a range of $195 billion and $205 billion.

The staggering AI bill overshadowed a strong quarter for Alphabet and added to pressures for Tesla, which beat on revenue but missed estimates for earnings per share.

"Tesla's and Alphabet's earnings are two sides of the same coin," stated cross-asset analyst Nic Puckrin. "Tech giants are spending on AI like there's no tomorrow, but investors have realized it's tomorrow they care about."

Alphabet delivered one of its strongest quarters in years. The market's reaction makes it clear, though, that investors are getting antsy to see a return on capex spending, especially as Alphabet's free cash flow goes negative for the first time in decades.

The big moves down in Tesla and Alphabet shares combined with a sharp jump in oil prices and bond yields, sending major indexes sharply lower in Thursday trading.

Here's where US indexes stood at the 4 p.m. ET closing bell on Thursday:

  • S&P 500: 7,408.15, down 1.21%
  • Dow Jones Industrial Average: 51,711.29, down 0.97% (-507.29 points)
  • Nasdaq 100: 28,454.81, down 1.87%

Several Wall Street banks lowered their Tesla stock price targets after earnings, though a number of analysts feel that the spending is necessary for the company to deliver on the initiatives it has been promising for years.

"We view the heavy expenditures as Tesla laying the foundation for its real-world AI—namely autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots," wrote Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein. "While these new ventures are unlikely to generate meaningful results in the near term, we see strong long-term growth for both products."

Morningstar analyst Malik Ahmed Khan shared a similar perspective on Alphabet due to its exposure to chips, infrastructure, and AI models, which he said justifies a bullish $415 price target, a 31% jump from Thursday's price.

Bob Lange, founder of Explosive Options, noted that while Google's capex estimates are dragging the stock down after earnings, the company faces a dilemma, as it's locked in a race for AI supremacy with its Big Tech rivals.

"I think the market is going to pretty soon understand that that there's there's going to be a limit to how much they can keep increasing capex, and this may be it right herem" he noted. "I don't think the market is appreciating the fact that every incremental dollar that they're spending in capex is going to be a huge return on their for their shareholders.

Following the report, Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco said he expects to see the stock ultimately recover, though he highlighted some concern regarding the cash burn described by Musk during the call, leading his team to scale back its price target

"We view Tesla's accelerating capex cycle as a necessary investment to secure leadership in autonomy & robotics," he wrote in a note to investors. "However, these investments push FCF further into negative territory, increasing focus on tangible Robotaxi & Optimus milestones. We lower our PT to $400 reflecting elevated cash burn."

blocks.html_en: Gone are the days when investors went along cheerily with Big Tech's plans to spend enormous sums on AI projects.

sentences.en: Gone are the days when investors went along cheerily with Big Tech's plans to spend enormous sums on AI projects.
sentences.zh: 投资者兴高采烈地支持大型科技公司斥巨资开发人工智能项目的日子已经一去不复返了。

blocks.html_en: That fact was on display with the precipitous post-earnings declines in shares of Tesla and Alphabet on Thursday. Elon Musk's EV maker saw its stock drop almost 15%, while the Google parent fell nearly 7%.

sentences.en: That fact was on display with the precipitous post-earnings declines in shares of Tesla and Alphabet on Thursday.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉和 Alphabet 周四财报公布后股价急剧下跌就证明了这一事实。

sentences.en: Elon Musk's EV maker saw its stock drop almost 15%, while the Google parent fell nearly 7%.
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的电动汽车制造商股价下跌近 15%，而谷歌母公司则下跌近 7%。

blocks.html_en: Tesla and Alphabet both reported earnings for the second quarter on Wednesday. Tesla boosted its capex projection for the year to over $25 billion, with Musk describing this year's spending as "massive" on the call with investors. Meanwhile, Alphabet bumped up its capex projection from $180 billion to $190 billion, to a range of $195 billion and $205 billion.

sentences.en: Tesla and Alphabet both reported earnings for the second quarter on Wednesday.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉和 Alphabet 周三均公布了第二季度财报。

sentences.en: Tesla boosted its capex projection for the year to over $25 billion, with Musk describing this year's spending as "massive" on the call with investors.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉将今年的资本支出预测提高至超过 250 亿美元，马斯克在与投资者的电话会议上称今年的支出“庞大”。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, Alphabet bumped up its capex projection from $180 billion to $190 billion, to a range of $195 billion and $205 billion.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，Alphabet 将资本支出预测从 1800 亿美元上调至 1900 亿美元，上调至 1950 亿美元至 2050 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: The staggering AI bill overshadowed a strong quarter for Alphabet and added to pressures for Tesla, which beat on revenue but missed estimates for earnings per share.

sentences.en: The staggering AI bill overshadowed a strong quarter for Alphabet and added to pressures for Tesla, which beat on revenue but missed estimates for earnings per share.
sentences.zh: 令人震惊的人工智能法案给 Alphabet 强劲的季度蒙上了阴影，并增加了特斯拉的压力，特斯拉的营收超出预期，但每股收益低于预期。

blocks.html_en: "Tesla's and Alphabet's earnings are two sides of the same coin," stated cross-asset analyst Nic Puckrin. "Tech giants are spending on AI like there's no tomorrow, but investors have realized it's tomorrow they care about."

sentences.en: "Tesla's and Alphabet's earnings are two sides of the same coin," stated cross-asset analyst Nic Puckrin.
sentences.zh: 跨资产分析师尼克·帕克林 (Nic Puckrin) 表示：“特斯拉和 Alphabet 的盈利是同一枚硬币的两个侧面。”

sentences.en: "Tech giants are spending on AI like there's no tomorrow, but investors have realized it's tomorrow they care about."
sentences.zh: “科技巨头在人工智能上的投资就像没有明天一样，但投资者已经意识到他们关心的是明天。”

blocks.html_en: Alphabet delivered one of its strongest quarters in years. The market's reaction makes it clear, though, that investors are getting antsy to see a return on capex spending, especially as Alphabet's free cash flow goes negative for the first time in decades.

sentences.en: Alphabet delivered one of its strongest quarters in years.
sentences.zh: Alphabet 迎来了多年来最强劲的季度之一。

sentences.en: The market's reaction makes it clear, though, that investors are getting antsy to see a return on capex spending, especially as Alphabet's free cash flow goes negative for the first time in decades.
sentences.zh: 不过，市场的反应清楚地表明，投资者对资本支出的回报感到不安，尤其是在 Alphabet 的自由现金流几十年来首次出现负值的情况下。

blocks.html_en: The big moves down in Tesla and Alphabet shares combined with a sharp jump in oil prices and bond yields, sending major indexes sharply lower in Thursday trading.

sentences.en: The big moves down in Tesla and Alphabet shares combined with a sharp jump in oil prices and bond yields, sending major indexes sharply lower in Thursday trading.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉和 Alphabet 股价大幅下跌，加上油价和债券收益率大幅上涨，导致主要股指在周四交易中大幅走低。

blocks.html_en: Here's where US indexes stood at the 4 p.m. ET closing bell on Thursday:

sentences.en: Here's where US indexes stood at the 4 p.m.
sentences.zh: 这是下午 4 点美国指数的走势。

sentences.en: ET closing bell on Thursday:
sentences.zh: 东部时间周四收盘：

blocks.html_en: • S&P 500: 7,408.15, down 1.21%
• Dow Jones Industrial Average: 51,711.29, down 0.97% (-507.29 points)

sentences.en: Dow Jones Industrial Average: 51,711.29, down 0.97% (-507.29 points)

blocks.html_en: • Nasdaq 100: 28,454.81, down 1.87%

Several Wall Street banks lowered their Tesla stock price targets after earnings, though a number of analysts feel that the spending is necessary for the company to deliver on the initiatives it has been promising for years.

sentences.en: Several Wall Street banks lowered their Tesla stock price targets after earnings, though a number of analysts feel that the spending is necessary for the company to deliver on the initiatives it has been promising for years.
sentences.zh: 几家华尔街银行在公布财报后下调了特斯拉的股价目标，不过许多分析师认为，这笔支出对于该公司实现其多年来承诺的举措是必要的。

blocks.html_en: "We view the heavy expenditures as Tesla laying the foundation for its real-world AI—namely autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots," wrote Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein. "While these new ventures are unlikely to generate meaningful results in the near term, we see strong long-term growth for both products."

sentences.en: "We view the heavy expenditures as Tesla laying the foundation for its real-world AI—namely autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots," wrote Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein.
sentences.zh: 晨星公司分析师塞斯·戈德斯坦（Seth Goldstein）写道：“我们认为，特斯拉的巨额支出是为其现实世界的人工智能（即自动驾驶汽车和人形机器人）奠定了基础。”

sentences.en: "While these new ventures are unlikely to generate meaningful results in the near term, we see strong long-term growth for both products."
sentences.zh: “虽然这些新企业不太可能在短期内产生有意义的成果，但我们看到这两种产品的长期强劲增长。”

blocks.html_en: Morningstar analyst Malik Ahmed Khan shared a similar perspective on Alphabet due to its exposure to chips, infrastructure, and AI models, which he said justifies a bullish $415 price target, a 31% jump from Thursday's price.

sentences.en: Morningstar analyst Malik Ahmed Khan shared a similar perspective on Alphabet due to its exposure to chips, infrastructure, and AI models, which he said justifies a bullish $415 price target, a 31% jump from Thursday's price.
sentences.zh: 晨星分析师马利克·艾哈迈德·汗 (Malik Ahmed Khan) 对 Alphabet 也持有类似的看法，因为该公司在芯片、基础设施和人工智能模型方面存在风险，他表示，这证明了看涨 415 美元的价格目标是合理的，比周四的价格上涨了 31%。

blocks.html_en: Bob Lange, founder of Explosive Options, noted that while Google's capex estimates are dragging the stock down after earnings, the company faces a dilemma, as it's locked in a race for AI supremacy with its Big Tech rivals.

sentences.en: Bob Lange, founder of Explosive Options, noted that while Google's capex estimates are dragging the stock down after earnings, the company faces a dilemma, as it's locked in a race for AI supremacy with its Big Tech rivals.
sentences.zh: Explosive Options 创始人鲍勃·兰格 (Bob Lange) 指出，虽然谷歌的资本支出预期拖累了该股在盈利后的下跌，但该公司面临着两难境地，因为它正与大型科技竞争对手争夺人工智能霸主地位。

blocks.html_en: "I think the market is going to pretty soon understand that that there's there's going to be a limit to how much they can keep increasing capex, and this may be it right herem" he noted. "I don't think the market is appreciating the fact that every incremental dollar that they're spending in capex is going to be a huge return on their for their shareholders.

sentences.en: "I think the market is going to pretty soon understand that that there's there's going to be a limit to how much they can keep increasing capex, and this may be it right herem" he noted.
sentences.zh: 他指出：“我认为市场很快就会明白，他们继续增加资本支出的程度将会受到限制，而这可能就是现在的情况。”

sentences.en: "I don't think the market is appreciating the fact that every incremental dollar that they're spending in capex is going to be a huge return on their for their shareholders.
sentences.zh: “我认为市场并没有意识到他们在资本支出上投入的每一美元都会为股东带来巨大的回报。

blocks.html_en: Following the report, Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco said he expects to see the stock ultimately recover, though he highlighted some concern regarding the cash burn described by Musk during the call, leading his team to scale back its price target

sentences.en: Following the report, Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco said he expects to see the stock ultimately recover, though he highlighted some concern regarding the cash burn described by Musk during the call, leading his team to scale back its price target
sentences.zh: 报告发布后，摩根士丹利分析师安德鲁·佩尔科科表示，他预计该股最终会反弹，尽管他强调了对马斯克在电话会议中描述的现金消耗的一些担忧，导致他的团队缩减了其价格目标

blocks.html_en: "We view Tesla's accelerating capex cycle as a necessary investment to secure leadership in autonomy & robotics," he wrote in a note to investors. "However, these investments push FCF further into negative territory, increasing focus on tangible Robotaxi & Optimus milestones. We lower our PT to $400 reflecting elevated cash burn."

sentences.en: "We view Tesla's accelerating capex cycle as a necessary investment to secure leadership in autonomy & robotics," he wrote in a note to investors.
sentences.zh: 他在给投资者的一份报告中写道：“我们认为特斯拉加速的资本支出周期是确保自动驾驶和机器人技术领先地位的必要投资。”

sentences.en: "However, these investments push FCF further into negative territory, increasing focus on tangible Robotaxi & Optimus milestones.
sentences.zh: “然而，这些投资将 FCF 进一步推向负值区域，越来越关注有形的 Robotaxi 和 Optimus 里程碑。

sentences.en: We lower our PT to $400 reflecting elevated cash burn."
sentences.zh: 我们将目标价降低至 400 美元，反映出现金消耗增加。”

sentences.en: At Business Insider, he covers important macro and micro economic stories, including takes from leading economists and hedge fund managers, breaking IPOs, corporate bankruptcies, meme stocks and short-selling.
sentences.zh: 在《商业内幕》中，他报道了重要的宏观和微观经济故事，包括顶尖经济学家和对冲基金经理的观点、首次公开募股失败、企业破产、迷因股票和卖空。

sentences.en: He also writes on other markets such as crypto, oil and real estate.He has interviewed many of the market’s most influential voices, ranging from top economists such as Mark Zandi and Richard Thalerto prominent investors including Danny Moses, Andrew Left, Anthony Scaramucci, Louis Navellier and Grant Cardone.Programs such as LiveNOW from Fox , Taking Stock and Ticker News have had Samuel on to discuss stock market and economic developments.
sentences.zh: 他还撰写有关加密货币、石油和房地产等其他市场的文章。他采访了许多市场上最有影响力的声音，其中包括马克·赞迪（Mark Zandi）和理查德·泰勒托（Richard Thalerto）等顶级经济学家，以及丹尼·摩西（Danny Moses）、安德鲁·莱夫特（Andrew Left）、安东尼·斯卡拉穆奇（Anthony Scaramucci）、路易斯·纳维利尔（Louis Navellier）和格兰特·卡登（Grant Cardone）等著名投资者。福克斯的 LiveNOW、Taking Stock 和 Ticker News 等节目都邀请塞缪尔讨论股市和经济发展。

sentences.en: His reporting has been cited by The New York Times DealBook, Bloomberg Radio, Forbes, Entrepreneur, Gizmodo and TheFutureParty.Samuel began at InvestorPlace, covering investing, retail trading and macro economic trends.
sentences.zh: 他的报道曾被纽约时报 DealBook、彭博电台、福布斯、Entrepreneur、Gizmodo 和 TheFutureParty 引用。Samuel 最初在 InvestorPlace 工作，报道投资、零售交易和宏观经济趋势。

sentences.en: Prior to joining Business Insider, he served as a technology markets reporter at TheStreet.
sentences.zh: 在加入 Business Insider 之前，他曾担任 TheStreet 的技术市场记者。

sentences.en: He is a graduate of Sarah Lawrence College and Trinity College Dublin.Samuel's work has appeared in publications such as TipRanks, EV and Observer.
sentences.zh: 他毕业于莎拉·劳伦斯学院和都柏林三一学院。Samuel 的作品曾发表在 TipRanks、EV 和 Observer 等出版物上。

sentences.en: When he isn't chasing down stories, he can often be found browsing book and record shops.
sentences.zh: 当他不追寻故事时，人们经常会发现他在逛书店和唱片店。

sentences.en: To reach Samuel, email him at sobrient@insider.com or connect with him on LinkedIn.
sentences.zh: 要联系 Samuel，请发送电子邮件至 sobrient@insider.com 或通过 LinkedIn 与他联系。

sentences.en: He is also on Signal as Samuel Clemens.
sentences.zh: 他还在《信号》中饰演塞缪尔·克莱门斯。

sentences.en: Popular Articles: A Nobel economist has a warning for meme stock tradersThe business school dropout who kicked off the Beyond Meat rally wants you to know he's not Roaring Kitty 2.0A top economist who thinks we're on the brink of a recession says he's eyeing these 3 warning signsTrump's 401(k) executive order marks big changes for retirement savings — and possibly puts your money at riskWhy hedge fund icon Ray Dalio says you shouldn't invest in real estate in this economyAI bullishness is soaring, but pros see a major opportunity brewing in an overlooked corner of the market
sentences.zh: 热门文章： 一位诺贝尔经济学家向 meme 股票交易员发出警告 发起 Beyond Meat 集会的商学院辍学者希望你知道他不是 Roaring Kitty 2.0 一位认为我们正处于衰退边缘的顶级经济学家表示，他正在关注这 3 个警告信号 特朗普的 401(k) 行政命令标志着退休储蓄的重大变化 - 可能会让你的钱面临风险 为什么对冲基金偶像雷·达利奥 (Ray Dalio) 说你不应该投资房地产在当前经济形势下，人工智能的乐观情绪正在飙升，但专业人士看到了市场中被忽视的角落正在酝酿的重大机遇

content_html_zh:
支出再次罢工：特斯拉和Alphabet因高额资本支出计划而受到投资者的惩罚

作者：Samuel O'Brient 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

投资者兴高采烈地支持大型科技公司斥巨资开发人工智能项目的日子已经一去不复返了。

特斯拉和 Alphabet 周四财报公布后股价急剧下跌就证明了这一事实。 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的电动汽车制造商股价下跌近 15%，而谷歌母公司则下跌近 7%。

特斯拉和 Alphabet 周三均公布了第二季度财报。 特斯拉将今年的资本支出预测提高至超过 250 亿美元，马斯克在与投资者的电话会议上称今年的支出“庞大”。 与此同时，Alphabet 将资本支出预测从 1800 亿美元上调至 1900 亿美元，上调至 1950 亿美元至 2050 亿美元。

令人震惊的人工智能法案给 Alphabet 强劲的季度蒙上了阴影，并增加了特斯拉的压力，特斯拉的营收超出预期，但每股收益低于预期。

跨资产分析师尼克·帕克林 (Nic Puckrin) 表示：“特斯拉和 Alphabet 的盈利是同一枚硬币的两个侧面。” “科技巨头在人工智能上的投资就像没有明天一样，但投资者已经意识到他们关心的是明天。”

Alphabet 迎来了多年来最强劲的季度之一。 不过，市场的反应清楚地表明，投资者对资本支出的回报感到不安，尤其是在 Alphabet 的自由现金流几十年来首次出现负值的情况下。

特斯拉和 Alphabet 股价大幅下跌，加上油价和债券收益率大幅上涨，导致主要股指在周四交易中大幅走低。

这是下午 4 点美国指数的走势。 东部时间周四收盘：

  • S&P 500: 7,408.15, down 1.21%
  • 道琼斯工业平均指数：51,711.29，下跌0.97%（-507.29点）
  • 纳斯达克100指数：28,454.81，下跌1.87%

几家华尔街银行在公布财报后下调了特斯拉的股价目标，不过许多分析师认为，这笔支出对于该公司实现其多年来承诺的举措是必要的。

晨星公司分析师塞斯·戈德斯坦（Seth Goldstein）写道：“我们认为，特斯拉的巨额支出是为其现实世界的人工智能（即自动驾驶汽车和人形机器人）奠定了基础。” “虽然这些新企业不太可能在短期内产生有意义的成果，但我们看到这两种产品的长期强劲增长。”

晨星分析师马利克·艾哈迈德·汗 (Malik Ahmed Khan) 对 Alphabet 也持有类似的看法，因为该公司在芯片、基础设施和人工智能模型方面存在风险，他表示，这证明了看涨 415 美元的价格目标是合理的，比周四的价格上涨了 31%。

Explosive Options 创始人鲍勃·兰格 (Bob Lange) 指出，虽然谷歌的资本支出预期拖累了该股在盈利后的下跌，但该公司面临着两难境地，因为它正与大型科技竞争对手争夺人工智能霸主地位。

他指出：“我认为市场很快就会明白，他们继续增加资本支出的程度将会受到限制，而这可能就是现在的情况。” “我认为市场并没有意识到他们在资本支出上投入的每一美元都会为股东带来巨大的回报。

报告发布后，摩根士丹利分析师安德鲁·佩尔科科表示，他预计该股最终会反弹，尽管他强调了对马斯克在电话会议中描述的现金消耗的一些担忧，导致他的团队缩减了其价格目标

他在给投资者的一份报告中写道：“我们认为特斯拉加速的资本支出周期是确保自动驾驶和机器人技术领先地位的必要投资。” “然而，这些投资将 FCF 进一步推向负值区域，越来越关注有形的 Robotaxi 和 Optimus 里程碑。 我们将目标价降低至 400 美元，反映出现金消耗增加。”

阅读下一篇

塞缪尔·奥布莱恩 (Samuel O'Brient) 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

塞缪尔·奥布莱恩 (Samuel O'Brient) 是一位经验丰富的金融市场和商业记者，撰写的文章涉及经济、技术和公共政策等广泛主题。 在《商业内幕》中，他报道了重要的宏观和微观经济故事，包括顶尖经济学家和对冲基金经理的观点、首次公开募股失败、企业破产、迷因股票和卖空。 他还撰写有关加密货币、石油和房地产等其他市场的文章。他采访了许多市场上最有影响力的声音，其中包括马克·赞迪（Mark Zandi）和理查德·泰勒托（Richard Thalerto）等顶级经济学家，以及丹尼·摩西（Danny Moses）、安德鲁·莱夫特（Andrew Left）、安东尼·斯卡拉穆奇（Anthony Scaramucci）、路易斯·纳维利尔（Louis Navellier）和格兰特·卡登（Grant Cardone）等著名投资者。福克斯的 LiveNOW、Taking Stock 和 Ticker News 等节目都邀请塞缪尔讨论股市和经济发展。 他的报道曾被纽约时报 DealBook、彭博电台、福布斯、Entrepreneur、Gizmodo 和 TheFutureParty 引用。Samuel 最初在 InvestorPlace 工作，报道投资、零售交易和宏观经济趋势。 在加入 Business Insider 之前，他曾担任 TheStreet 的技术市场记者。 他毕业于莎拉·劳伦斯学院和都柏林三一学院。Samuel 的作品曾发表在 TipRanks、EV 和 Observer 等出版物上。 当他不追寻故事时，人们经常会发现他在逛书店和唱片店。 要联系 Samuel，请发送电子邮件至 sobrient@insider.com 或通过 LinkedIn 与他联系。 他还在《信号》中饰演塞缪尔·克莱门斯。 热门文章： 一位诺贝尔经济学家向 meme 股票交易员发出警告 发起 Beyond Meat 集会的商学院辍学者希望你知道他不是 Roaring Kitty 2.0 一位认为我们正处于衰退边缘的顶级经济学家表示，他正在关注这 3 个警告信号 特朗普的 401(k) 行政命令标志着退休储蓄的重大变化 - 可能会让你的钱面临风险 为什么对冲基金偶像雷·达利奥 (Ray Dalio) 说你不应该投资房地产在当前经济形势下，人工智能的乐观情绪正在飙升，但专业人士看到了市场中被忽视的角落正在酝酿的重大机遇
